title: uncaria lanosa var. ferrea
intro: Uncaria lanosa var. ferrea, also known as the "Rahu" or "Snake Vine," is a variety of the Uncaria lanosa plant, a species of climbing vine native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating joint pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Uncaria lanosa var. ferrea is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be found in powdered or capsule form, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human use.
